description: Replace single-agent deck builder with three modes: Strategist (deck structure), Art Director (creative design with QA), Page Builder (fast HTML editing like Claude Code). Tab bar UI, mode-specific tools/prompts/models, Fine-tune button transitions.

userStories.title: Mode type definitions and configuration registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed mode system that defines the configuration for each mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/deck-builder/agent-modes.ts
Export AgentMode type: 'strategist' | 'art-director' | 'page-builder'
Export AgentModeConfig interface with: mode, label, description, model, icon, placeholder (chat input text)
Export AGENT_MODES: Record<AgentMode, AgentModeConfig> with configs for all three modes
Strategist: model claude-sonnet-4-20250514, label 'Strategy', icon '📋', placeholder 'Describe your deck structure...'
Art Director: model claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (default, complex uses opus), label 'Design', icon '🎨', placeholder 'Describe the design you want...'
Page Builder: model claude-sonnet-4-20250514, label 'Build', icon '⚡', placeholder 'What should I change on this slide?'
Export detectModeIntent(message: string, currentMode: AgentMode): AgentMode | null — regex-based detection
Strategist signals: restructure, reorder, add a slide, remove slide, deck flow, storyline, outline, build me a deck
Page Builder signals: change the font, make it Npx, edit the heading, fix the padding, move this div, change background to
Art Director signals: redesign, make it more, improve this, show me options, make this impactful
Returns null if message fits current mode
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation for everything else. Read src/lib/deck-builder/agents/orchestrator-tools.ts for existing tool definitions. Read src/lib/deck-builder/model-router.ts for current model routing patterns. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Strategist system prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a system prompt for the Strategist mode focused on deck structure and narrative flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/deck-builder/agents/strategist-prompt.ts
Export getStrategistPrompt(context) — context includes deck name, description, slide outline (array of {index, title}), slide count
Prompt persona: Senior presentation strategist. McKinsey-trained. You structure the story.
Prompt includes full deck outline with all slide titles and indices
Prompt explicitly says: You CANNOT modify slide HTML, CSS, or visual design. You work with titles, key messages, and slide order.
Prompt says: When deck is empty or user asks for new deck, create complete outline with titles and key messages
Execution rules: act first, explain briefly, no filler, max 2 sentences per action
Includes CURRENT STATE block with deck outline (titles only, no HTML)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read src/lib/deck-builder/agents/orchestrator-prompt.ts for the existing prompt pattern (structured state block, execution rules). Model after that but strip all design/HTML references. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Page Builder system prompt
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a system prompt for Page Builder mode focused on precise HTML/CSS execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/deck-builder/agents/page-builder-prompt.ts
Export getPageBuilderPrompt(context) — context includes slide index, slide HTML (full source), theme colors/fonts, media refs
Prompt persona: Expert frontend developer. You execute exactly what the user asks. You are their hands on the keyboard.
Prompt includes current slide's FULL HTML source in the context
Prompt says: Execute the user's instruction precisely. Do NOT redesign or reinterpret.
Prompt says: For small changes use patch_slide_html. For large rewrites use set_slide_html.
Prompt says: No QA needed. The user is looking at the slide and will tell you if something is wrong.
Prompt includes media proxy URL pattern: /api/media/s3?key=PATH
Prompt includes CDN scripts and Google Fonts loading instructions
Prompt says: You can search for dataset samples to find media to embed.
Includes CURRENT STATE block with slide HTML (first 2000 chars), media_refs, theme
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This prompt should feel like Claude Code's system prompt — precise, code-focused, no design opinions. Read src/lib/deck-builder/agents/design-prompt.ts for the media URL and CDN patterns to include. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: patch_slide_html tool for surgical HTML edits
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a surgical HTML patch tool for Page Builder that makes targeted changes without full rewrites.
acceptanceCriteria: Add patch_slide_html tool definition in a new section of ai-tools.ts (or orchestrator-tools.ts)
Tool params: { slide_index: number, patches: Array<{ selector: string, action: 'replace' | 'setAttribute' | 'setStyle' | 'setText' | 'remove', value?: string }> }
Handler: for each patch, use regex to find elements matching the selector and apply the action
setStyle: parse existing style attribute, merge new style properties, write back
setText: replace innerHTML/text content of matched element
replace: replace entire matched element with new HTML from value
setAttribute: set or update an attribute on matched element
remove: remove matched element entirely
Returns updated slides array with patched HTML
If selector doesn't match, include error in result string but don't fail — continue with other patches
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Keep it simple — regex-based, not a full DOM parser. No jsdom dependency. Common selectors to support: tag names (h1, div, p), class selectors (.heading, .stats), id selectors (#title). Don't need to support complex CSS selectors. Read src/lib/deck-builder/ai-tools.ts processToolCall for handler pattern. Use python-backend-expert or general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Mode-specific tool sets
userStories.description: As a developer, I need getModeTools() to return the correct filtered tool array for each mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Add getModeTools(mode: AgentMode): Anthropic.Tool[] to agent-modes.ts
Strategist tools: get_all_slides, add_slide, delete_slide, reorder_slides, restructure_deck, edit_slide (title/body only), generate_section, delegate_research, get_deck_approach, load_skill, think

Strategist EXCLUDES: delegate_design, delegate_qa, set_slide_html, patch_slide_html, get_slide_html, set_template_theme, customize_theme, generate_variations, generate_image, generate_video, apply_to_all_slides
Art Director tools: ALL current ORCHESTRATOR_TOOLS (unchanged from current system)
Page Builder tools: get_slide_html, set_slide_html, patch_slide_html, edit_slide (all fields), get_media_assets, get_site_media, delegate_research, think
Page Builder EXCLUDES: delegate_design, delegate_qa, generate_variations, add_slide, delete_slide, reorder_slides, restructure_deck, generate_section, get_deck_approach, load_skill
Import tool definitions from orchestrator-tools.ts and filter by name
For strategist edit_slide: create a modified version with field enum restricted to ['title', 'body']
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read src/lib/deck-builder/agents/orchestrator-tools.ts for ORCHESTRATOR_TOOLS array. Filter by tool.name. The strategist edit_slide needs a cloned tool definition with modified input_schema. Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Mode-aware chat route — accept mode param and route config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the chat route to accept a mode parameter and select the right prompt, tools, and model.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify POST /api/admin/deck-builder/[id]/chat request body to accept mode: AgentMode (optional, default 'art-director')
Import AgentMode, AGENT_MODES, getModeTools from agent-modes.ts
Import getStrategistPrompt and getPageBuilderPrompt
Based on mode, select system prompt: strategist -> getStrategistPrompt, art-director -> getOrchestratorPrompt, page-builder -> getPageBuilderPrompt
Based on mode, select tools: getModeTools(mode)
Based on mode, select model: use AGENT_MODES[mode].model (override existing routeModel for non-art-director modes)
Page Builder mode: inject full slide HTML into the prompt context (selected slide's html field)
Page Builder mode: skip QA pipeline entirely — no callQAAgent, no autoVerifySlide, no forceStopAfterResult
Strategist mode: skip design delegation — if delegate_design is somehow called, return error
Emit { type: 'mode_info', mode: string } stream event at start of response
Save mode in chat message metadata_json: { mode: 'page-builder', ... }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core wiring. The chat route is at src/app/api/admin/deck-builder/[id]/chat/route.ts (~850 lines). The key changes are at the top of the handler: select prompt/tools/model based on mode. Then in the delegation section: page-builder skips QA, strategist blocks design delegation. Art Director mode is unchanged (current behavior). Use general-purpose agent.

userStories.title: Mode tab bar UI component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and switch between Strategy/Design/Build modes via a tab bar above the chat panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Add mode state to ChatPanel: const [mode, setMode] = useState<AgentMode>('art-director')
Create ModeTabBar sub-component (or inline in ChatPanel) with three tabs
Three tabs: Strategy (📋) | Design (🎨) | Build (⚡)
Active tab: sage green (#92B090) border-bottom-2 + accent text color
Inactive tabs: muted text (var(--text-muted)), hover shows lighter text
Tabs use font-mono, text-[11px], compact height (~28px total)
Tab bar sits between the chat header ('AI Chat' + '[new chat]') and the messages area
Clicking tab calls setMode() and does NOT clear chat history
When mode changes, insert a subtle divider in messages: '— switched to Build mode —' in muted text
Pass mode to sendMessage so it's included in the API POST body
Chat input placeholder changes per mode from AGENT_MODES config
Import AgentMode and AGENT_MODES from agent-modes.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Read src/app/components/deck-builder/ChatPanel.tsx for the existing chat UI structure. The tab bar goes after the header div (the one with 'AI Chat' and '[new chat]') and before the messages scroll area. Match the terminal/monospace aesthetic. Use frontend-expert agent for clean component design. Use Shadcn UI Designer agent if helpful for tab component patterns.

userStories.title: Mode-specific suggestion chips
userStories.description: As a user, I want suggestion chips to change based on current mode so I know what the mode can do.
acceptanceCriteria: Create SUGGESTION_CHIPS_BY_MODE config object in ChatPanel.tsx (or agent-modes.ts)
Strategist chips: 'Build a sales deck', 'Restructure the flow', 'Add a closing slide', 'Improve the narrative'
Art Director chips: 'Redesign this slide', 'Make it more impactful', 'Add data visualization', 'Show me 3 options'
Page Builder chips: 'Make heading bigger', 'Add video grid', 'Change background color', 'Fix the spacing'
Chips update immediately when mode tab is clicked (no re-render delay)
Replace existing hardcoded SUGGESTION_CHIPS with mode-aware version
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Read ChatPanel.tsx for the existing SUGGESTION_CHIPS constant (around line 59). Replace with a Record<AgentMode, string[]> and index by current mode. Use frontend-expert agent.

userStories.title: Fine-tune button on QA results
userStories.description: As a user, after seeing a design result with QA, I want a Fine-tune button that switches to Page Builder mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Fine-tune' button to the QA action buttons in ChatPanel.tsx (alongside Accept/Improve/Redo)
Button uses ⚡ icon and 'Fine-tune' label
Button style: matches existing action button pattern but uses a distinct color (e.g., blue-ish or the Build mode accent)
Clicking Fine-tune: sets mode to 'page-builder' via setMode callback
Inserts a mode-switch divider in the chat: '— switched to Build mode —'
Chat input gets focus so user can immediately type
The mode tab bar reflects the change (Build tab active)
Fine-tune button appears for BOTH pass and needs_work QA results
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Read ChatPanel.tsx around line 693 for the existing QA action buttons (qaActions rendering). The Fine-tune button goes alongside Accept/Improve/Redo. It needs access to setMode which should be passed down or available in scope. Use frontend-expert agent.

userStories.title: Natural language mode detection suggestion chip
userStories.description: As a user, when I type something that belongs to a different mode, I want a suggestion to switch.
acceptanceCriteria: In ChatPanel sendMessage, before sending to API, call detectModeIntent(message, currentMode)
If result is non-null (different mode detected), show a suggestion chip above the chat input
Chip text: 'This sounds like a [Build] task. Switch?' with the mode label and icon
Chip has two buttons: 'Switch' (changes mode and sends) and 'Keep' (sends in current mode)
Chip disappears after any action (switch, keep, or new message typed)
Detection is lightweight — regex only, no LLM call
If user clicks Switch, mode changes AND message sends in the new mode
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: detectModeIntent is defined in US-001 agent-modes.ts. This story is the UI rendering. The suggestion chip should feel unobtrusive — a small bar between the input and messages, not a modal or dialog. Use frontend-expert agent.

userStories.title: Playwright E2E test — mode switching flow
userStories.description: As QA, I need an end-to-end test verifying mode switching works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/deck-agent-modes.spec.ts
Log in as admin (team@claru.ai / qweqwe123!)
Navigate to deck builder with template 54f7930f-cf81-4c0d-8f66-28ddaf93476a
Verify default mode is Design (tab highlighted with accent color)
Click Strategy tab, verify it becomes active
Verify suggestion chips change to strategy-related options
Click Build tab, verify it becomes active
Verify suggestion chips change to build-related options
Verify chat input placeholder changes to 'What should I change on this slide?'
Click Design tab, verify mode switches back
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Dev server at http://localhost:3005. Use login pattern from tests/e2e/deck-builder-crud.spec.ts. This test does NOT send messages to the API (avoids ANTHROPIC_API_KEY requirement). Just tests UI state changes. Use webapp-testing skill with Playwright MCP.

userStories.title: Playwright E2E test — Page Builder speed
userStories.description: As QA, I need a test verifying Page Builder mode responds faster than Art Director mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/deck-page-builder-speed.spec.ts
Test requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (skip if not set)
Log in as admin, navigate to deck builder
Switch to Build mode
Send 'change the title to Test Title'
Measure time from send to done event
Assert response time < 10 seconds
Timeout: 30 seconds for the full test
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This test validates that Page Builder mode doesn't trigger the heavy design→QA pipeline. The 10s threshold is generous — target is 2-5s. Use webapp-testing skill with Playwright MCP.
